title: acaena glabra
intro: Acaena glabra, also known as the hairy cat's ear, is a perennial flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in bioactive compounds, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Acaena glabra can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
